correction: v0.3.1 replaced the earlier invented single FLOOD_ELECTRICAL_BOS bucket (~$217,279/MWdc) and PENDING status with the canonical flood_solar cell (8 named failure units + real depth->DR ordinates) from Damage_Modeling.
notes: solar_flood crosswalk is RESOLVED_FROM_DAMAGE_MODELING: the canonical flood_solar cell exists and is used verbatim (failure-unit IDs, value shares, depth->DR ordinates).

Flood grain is 8 named failure units (mostly electrical): FS_INV, FS_SWG, FS_XFMR, FS_COMB, FS_SCADA, FS_CABLE, FS_FOUND, FS_PVMOD; NOT PV module glass. Value ladder reused from solar_hail because it is peril-agnostic.
Only 3 of 34 benchmark rows normalize to a $/MW basis (DEPCOM avoided-loss, VDE/AXIS portfolio aggregate, Valdora measured zero). Isolated solar-flood $/MW physical severity is genuinely scarce.

The flood_solar curves are public-source-anchored engineering parameterizations (DOE/FEMP, NEMA, FEMA, USACE HEC-FIA), NOT claims-calibrated; the cell owns severity curves only (EAL/PML/VaR/TVaR are downstream).
Row counts above equal the actual data-row counts of the corresponding CSVs and the source_registry.json length.
